name: Pakistan
avoid: July–September can bring monsoon flooding in the plains and landslides on northern mountain roads.
Karachi is hottest and most humid from late April to June.
Skardu and upper Hunza flights are weather-sensitive; keep a buffer day before international departures.

cities.name: Islamabad
cities.notes: Faisal Mosque, leafy Margalla foothills, Saidpur heritage lanes and Pakistan Monument viewpoints

cities.name: Lahore
cities.notes: Badshahi Mosque, Lahore Fort, old-city food streets and Mughal gardens

cities.name: Hunza Valley
cities.notes: Karimabad bazaars, Baltit Fort, Attabad Lake and Passu cone panoramas

cities.name: Skardu
cities.notes: Cold Desert dunes, Kharpocho Fort, Shangrila lakes and gateway to Baltistan

cities.name: Swat Valley
cities.notes: Mingora bazaars, Malam Jabba slopes, White Palace and river valleys

cities.name: Karachi
cities.notes: Arabian Sea sunsets, colonial Saddar, mausoleums and iconic Bun Kabab stops

itinerary.sim: Jazz and Zong are the easiest prepaid picks; expect roughly ₹180–₹240 for the SIM and ₹450–₹750 for a 20–30GB data bundle booked at the airport or major city franchise.

Lahore.name: Lahore
Lahore.note: Two days cover Lahore's essential Mughal monuments, the Walled City food circuit and an evening Wagah border outing.

days.theme: Walled City Icons, Fort Ramparts & Food Street

activities.name: Lahore Fort (Shahi Qila)
activities.tip: Foreigner pricing is usually PKR 500; carry small notes because counters often struggle with change.

activities.name: Badshahi Mosque courtyard and prayer hall
activities.tip: Dress modestly and avoid the main prayer hall during prayer times.

activities.name: Wazir Khan Mosque and Delhi Gate lanes
Shahi Hammam museum stop
Evening dinner on Fort Road Food Street

days.theme: Museum Day, Shalimar Gardens & Wagah Ceremony

activities.name: Lahore Museum
Shalimar Gardens
Anarkali Bazaar and Mall Road heritage walk
Wagah Border flag-lowering ceremony shared taxi
Gawalmandi dessert stop for falooda or kulfi

Islamabad.name: Islamabad
Islamabad.note: Two days are enough for Islamabad's marquee sights plus an easy Margalla and Saidpur loop before heading north.

days.theme: Capital Landmarks, Faisal Mosque & Pakistan Monument

activities.name: Daewoo Express or Faisal Movers from Lahore to Islamabad
activities.tip: Executive seats save time on roadside stops and are worth the small upgrade.

activities.name: Faisal Mosque
activities.tip: Visit late afternoon for the cleanest light and cooler marble courtyards.

activities.name: Pakistan Monument Museum
Daman-e-Koh viewpoint
Blue Area chai stop and Jinnah Super evening walk

days.theme: Lok Virsa, Saidpur Village & Margalla Nature

activities.name: Lok Virsa Heritage Museum
Saidpur Village heritage walk
Margalla Hills Trail 3 or Trail 5
Rawalpindi Raja Bazaar rickshaw hop
Monal trailhead sunset tea stop

Swat Valley.name: Swat Valley
Swat Valley.note: Two days balance Mingora and Marghazar heritage with a full Malam Jabba alpine outing.

days.theme: Islamabad to Mingora, Fizagat Riverfront & White Palace

activities.name: Faisal Movers or Daewoo-class coach from Islamabad to Mingora
Fizagat Park riverside walk
White Palace Marghazar
Mingora Bazaar shopping for shawls and dry fruit
Evening chapli kebab dinner

days.theme: Malam Jabba Slopes, Chairlift & Valley Viewpoints

activities.name: Malam Jabba chairlift or zipline area
activities.tip: Winter ski rentals cost extra; in summer, pay only for lift and adventure rides you actually use.

activities.name: Malam Jabba scenic chair ride viewpoints
Roadside trout lunch near Bahrain or Malam Jabba
Janjal picnic stop or orchard walk on the return drive
Late-evening bus prep for the Karakoram leg

Hunza Valley.name: Hunza Valley
Hunza Valley.note: Two days in Karimabad let you cover the heritage forts and the classic Attabad–Passu scenic loop without rushing.

days.theme: CPEC / Karakoram Highway Arrival, Karimabad & Baltit Fort

activities.name: Private coaster or shared transport from Swat/Islamabad into Hunza via the CPEC-upgraded Karakoram Highway
activities.tip: Break the run in Besham or Gilgit if you are not used to 12+ hour mountain road days.

activities.name: Baltit Fort, Karimabad
activities.tip: Recent local ticket pricing is PKR 500 for Pakistani nationals; guided fort slots add context and move fast in peak season.

activities.name: Karimabad bazaar walk and zero-point valley overlook
Altit Fort and royal garden
Sunset at Duikar / Eagle's Nest viewpoint
activities.cost: Free + ₹300–₹600 shared jeep if you skip the hike

days.theme: Attabad Lake, Passu Cones & Glacier Viewpoints

activities.name: Attabad Lake boat ride
Passu Cathedral / Passu Cones viewpoints
Hussaini Suspension Bridge approach stop
activities.cost: ₹60–₹120 pp parking or local upkeep fees

activities.name: Hopper Glacier or Ghulkin village jeep outing
Walnut cake and sea-buckthorn drink break at Café de Hunza

Skardu.name: Skardu
Skardu.note: Two days cover Skardu's classic forts, desert dunes and lake circuit while still leaving time for a weather buffer.

days.theme: Jaglot Road Transfer, Kharpocho Fort & Katpana Dunes

activities.name: Hunza to Skardu shared coaster or private jeep via Jaglot-Skardu Road
Kharpocho (Skardu) Fort
Katpana Cold Desert sunset walk
Skardu Bazaar dry-fruit and apricot shopping
Balti dinner in the old bazaar
Shangrila Resort / Lower Kachura Lake day visit
Satpara Lake boat or viewpoint stop
Organic Village walk or Sarfaranga view drive
Kachura trout lunch
Airport-area tea stop before the flight buffer night

Karachi.name: Karachi
Karachi.note: Two days give you the core colonial-and-coast circuit plus enough time to do Karachi's classic restaurant crawl.

days.theme: PIA Flight South, Mausoleum & Clifton Seafront

activities.name: PIA or Airblue flight from Skardu to Karachi via Islamabad
activities.tip: Keep at least half a day of buffer because northern departures often slide on weather.

activities.name: Mazar-e-Quaid (Quaid-e-Azam Mausoleum)
Frere Hall lawns and Saddar colonial walk
Clifton Beach sunset
Do Darya dinner strip

days.theme: Mohatta Palace, Street Food & Seaview Loop

activities.name: Mohatta Palace Museum
Empress Market and Zainab Market shopping loop
Pakistan Air Force Museum
Seaview camel ride or beach ATV area
Burns Road dessert crawl for rabri, falooda and bun kabab

connections.method: Daewoo Express or Faisal Movers coach on the M-2 Motorway (4.5–5.5 hr) or a short domestic flight
Daewoo/Faisal Movers-style coach to Mingora (4.5–6 hr) plus local taxi onward to Malam Jabba or Marghazar
Return toward the Hazara/CPEC corridor, then continue on the Karakoram Highway by shared coaster or private car with an optional Gilgit overnight
Shared coaster or private jeep via Gilgit and Jaglot-Skardu Road (7–10 hr depending on roadwork)
PIA / Airblue connection via Islamabad; use road only if weather cancels flights and you have 2 extra buffer days

extras: October to March is the easiest window for Lahore, Islamabad and Karachi, while April to June gives the cleanest mountain roads and views for Swat, Hunza and Skardu.
Indian rupees are not a practical payment currency inside Pakistan, so budget in rupees for planning only and withdraw or exchange into PKR after arrival.
Book PIA or Airblue northern sectors early and keep screenshot copies of tickets because weather cancellations are common in Gilgit-Baltistan.
Daewoo Express is strongest on intercity plains routes; once you head into Swat, Hunza or Skardu, expect to switch to Faisal Movers, NATCO, coasters or private jeeps.
Carry layers even in June because Hunza and Skardu evenings get cold, while Karachi stays humid enough that light cotton clothes matter year-round.
